verdict.answer_first: A's first paragraph is a complete summary: "TLS gives the connection three guarantees at once — confidentiality (eavesdroppers see only ciphertext), integrity (tampering is detected), and authentication (you're really talking to the server named in the URL, not an imposter)."
verdict.decisiveness: A provides a complete explanation and concludes with a firm summary, whereas B ends by asking a question: "Want me to go deeper on any piece...?"
verdict.actionability: A is more actionable because its "Why this resists the obvious attacks" section directly maps the mechanisms to the threats a developer worries about, making the model easier to apply.
